text: Git branching is an essential part of version control that helps developers work on code without interfering with the main project. When creating a new feature, developers often use feature branches, which are separate copies of the code. This allows them to experiment safely. Once the feature is complete, it can be integrated back through a process called merging. Before merging, many teams use pull requests to review the changes, discuss improvements, and ensure quality. This creates a clear workflow, keeping the main branch stable while enabling collaboration. Understanding branches makes teamwork more organized and reduces the risk of errors.
